concerns.text: T009 and T010 both target code/data/validator.py with [P] parallel tags, but they write to the same file (validator.py). This creates implicit shared-state dependency — not truly parallel-safe. FR-009 and FR-002 flagging systems must be implemented in sequence or split into separate modules.

T043 (Phase 6) duplicates T009 (Phase 2) — both implement 'flagging system for missing invariant flags' in code/data/validator.py. This creates ordering violation: consumer (T043) appears after producer (T009) already implemented the same artifact. T043 should be removed or merged.

T065 and T066 appear in BOTH Phase 2 (Foundational) AND Phase N (Polish) with identical IDs. This violates single-assignment discipline and creates ordering ambiguity. Phase N tasks should have new IDs (T067+, T068+) or be removed if already completed in Phase 2.

T026-T027 validate core invariants (crossing number, braid index) against KnotInfo, but FR-003 and SC-008 explicitly state core invariants are TABULATED from Knot Atlas (not computed) and algorithm validation applies only to additional invariants in Phase 2+. This creates semantic ordering violation: tasks attempt validation on artifacts that don't exist per spec.

T041 (source independence assessment) is placed in Phase 5 (regression analysis) but FR-013 requires this documentation in docs/reproducibility/hyperbolic_volume_validation.md, which should be produced during data validation (Phase 3/US1), not regression (Phase 5/US3). Consumer (US3) depends on artifact from earlier phase.

T049 generates 'logs.md' documentation file but FR-007 requires actual timestamped log files capturing operation/input/output/parameters/status/duration_ms for reproducibility. Task orders documentation before artifact production — violates producer-before-consumer flow.
Tasks T062-T066 (reviewer concerns) are marked [P] in Phase N but T065-T066 duplicate Foundational IDs. Re-number to T067+ and verify true parallel safety (different files, no shared state).
